subject: Geology
title: How Coal Forms
description: Learn How Coal Forms in one minute.

hook.text: A swamp 300 million years ago buried dead plants under mud. Heat and pressure slowly turned them into coal.

buildup.text: Coal forms from accumulated plant material buried and compressed over millions of years. It progresses through stages.

discovery.text: Peat becomes lignite, then bituminous coal, then anthracite. Each stage has higher carbon content and energy.

twist.text: Most coal formed during the Carboniferous Period. Fungi that decompose wood had not yet fully evolved then.

climax.text: Coal powered the Industrial Revolution and still generates electricity. Its geology shaped modern civilization.

quiz.question: What is the highest grade of coal?
